evals.prompt:
Write a formal ACM/IEEE computing paper from these survived findings. Topic: 'energy efficiency of large language model inference'. Include all required sections (Abstract, Introduction, Related Work, Approach/System Design, Evaluation, Discussion, Conclusion & Future Work, References) and CCS Concepts / keywords front matter. Use IEEE numbered [1]-style citations resolving to a numbered reference list. Write the paper to computing-paper.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_energy_1 | title: Flash attention 2 reduces GPU memory bandwidth consumption by 37% on A100 hardware | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.08691 | author: Dao | year: 2023
- id: urn:mif:f_energy_2 | title: Mixture-of-experts routing reduces average activated parameters to 12-15% of total model size | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.04088 | author: Jiang et al. | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_energy_3 | title: Transformer inference energy scales approximately quadratically with sequence length at current hardware utilization rates | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/scaling-energy-2026 | author: Chen et al. | year: 2026
evals.expected_output: An ACM/IEEE computing paper with all sections in order: Abstract (150-250 words), Introduction, Related Work, Approach/System Design, Evaluation (experimental setup + results), Discussion, Conclusion & Future Work, References. CCS Concepts / keywords front matter. IEEE numbered bracket citations in text (e.g., '[1]') resolving to a numbered reference list. No raw urn:mif: identifiers in prose. Hedged, qualified claims at academic altitude.

deterministic_checks.description: Required Related Work section present (ACM/IEEE-distinct section)
Required Approach / System Design / Method section present
Required Conclusion section present (Conclusion & Future Work)
Required Future Work present (Conclusion & Future Work)
CCS Concepts / keywords front matter present
At least one citation URL from the MIF findings appears in the References section
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose or body text
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers or internal finding IDs appear in the prose
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:' and 'f_energy_' must not appear as visible body text. Internal MIF IDs are resolved to IEEE numbered [N] citations — never exposed to the reader.

expectations.description: Citations are IEEE numbered, not author-date
expectations.criteria: In-text citations use numbered brackets ([1], [2], ...) ordered by first appearance, resolving to a numbered reference list. Author-date '(Dao, 2023)' style is NOT used.

expectations.description: The Evaluation section states experimental setup and results
expectations.criteria: The Evaluation section describes setup (datasets/baselines/metrics/environment) and reports results, rather than collapsing into a single unstructured paragraph.

evals.prompt:
Produce a formal ACM/IEEE computing paper on 'agentic AI system failure modes'. The finding set includes one falsified finding and one weakened finding — handle each according to the genre rules. Use IEEE numbered [1]-style citations. Include CCS Concepts / keywords. Write the paper to computing-paper.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_agent_1 | title: Prompt injection attacks succeed against 67% of tested autonomous agents in red-team evaluations by Perez and Ribeiro | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/prompt-injection-study | author: Perez and Ribeiro | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_agent_2 | title: Agentic systems exhibit goal drift in 100% of extended autonomous runs exceeding 48 hours with no human checkpoint | dimension: behavioral | verdict: falsified | citation: https://example.com/goal-drift-claim | author: Huang et al. | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_agent_3 | title: Tool-call error rates increase by approximately 3x when context window utilization exceeds 80% of capacity | dimension: technical | verdict: weakened | citation: https://example.com/context-degradation-study | author: Kim et al. | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: ACM/IEEE computing paper that: (1) excludes the falsified finding (f_agent_2 / 100% goal drift claim — must not appear in Evaluation), (2) includes the weakened finding (f_agent_3) but annotates it as weakened/uncertain, (3) includes the survived finding (f_agent_1) as settled evidence, (4) maintains the required sections including Related Work, Evaluation, and Conclusion & Future Work, (5) uses IEEE numbered [N] citations.

deterministic_checks.description: Survived finding f_agent_1 (Perez and Ribeiro prompt injection study) appears in the paper
Weakened finding f_agent_3 (Kim et al. context degradation) appears in the paper (weakened = include with annotation, not exclude)
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: The falsified finding (Huang et al., 100% goal drift) does NOT appear in the Evaluation as evidence
expectations.criteria: The claim that agentic systems exhibit goal drift in 100% of runs must not appear as a supported result. The Huang et al. 2025 study and the '100%' goal-drift figure must be absent from the Evaluation. The falsified finding may only be mentioned in the methodology (describing exclusion policy) — never as evidence.

expectations.description: The weakened finding (Kim et al., 3x tool-call error rate) carries an explicit uncertainty qualifier
expectations.criteria: The Kim et al. finding must carry an inline qualifier such as 'weakened evidence suggests', 'preliminary', 'contested', 'weakened verdict', or similar — not stated with the same confidence as the survived Perez/Ribeiro finding.

expectations.description: Citations are IEEE numbered, not author-date
expectations.criteria: In-text citations use numbered brackets ([1], [2], ...) resolving to a numbered reference list. Author-date style is NOT used.

evals.prompt:
Write an ACM/IEEE computing paper from these findings. Topic: 'retrieval-augmented generation accuracy benchmarks'. All four findings survived falsification. Citations are mandatory — every claim must trace to a finding via an IEEE numbered [N] citation. Include Related Work, an Evaluation with experimental setup and results, Conclusion & Future Work, and CCS Concepts / keywords. Write the paper to computing-paper.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_rag_1 | title: RAGAS framework achieves 0.89 faithfulness score on HotpotQA when top-3 chunks are retrieved | dimension: evaluation | verdict: survived | citation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.15217 | author: Es et al. | year: 2023
- id: urn:mif:f_rag_2 | title: Hybrid sparse-dense retrieval outperforms dense-only retrieval by 7-12 percentage points on multi-hop questions | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/hybrid-retrieval-2025 | author: Zhao and Park | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_rag_3 | title: Chunk size of 256 tokens outperforms 512-token chunks on precision for fact-dense technical documents | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/chunking-ablation | author: Nakamura et al. | year: 2024
- id: urn:mif:f_rag_4 | title: Re-ranking with a cross-encoder adds 4-6% absolute NDCG improvement over bi-encoder retrieval alone | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/reranking-study | author: Fernandez | year: 2026
evals.expected_output: A complete ACM/IEEE computing paper with all required sections. Every factual claim in the Evaluation traces to one of the four supplied findings with an IEEE numbered [N] citation. No uncited assertions. No urn:mif: leakage in prose. Numbered References section lists all four sources with URLs, ordered by first appearance.

deterministic_checks.description: Approach / System Design / Method section present
Finding f_rag_1 (RAGAS faithfulness score) referenced in the paper
Finding f_rag_2 (hybrid retrieval) referenced in the paper
Finding f_rag_3 (chunk size ablation) referenced in the paper
Finding f_rag_4 (re-ranking NDCG improvement) referenced in the paper
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the body text or Evaluation section
No internal MIF finding ID handles leak into the output body

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers or f_rag_N internal IDs appear in the body text
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:', 'f_rag_1', 'f_rag_2', 'f_rag_3', 'f_rag_4' must not appear as visible prose. All references are IEEE numbered [N] citations.

expectations.description: The numbered References section lists all four sources with their URLs, ordered by first appearance
expectations.criteria: The References section contains numbered entries for Es et al. 2023 (arxiv.org/abs/2309.15217), Zhao and Park 2025, Nakamura et al. 2024, and Fernandez 2026, each with the citation URL, numbered in order of first in-text appearance.

expectations.description: The Discussion or Conclusion names at least one genuine limitation rather than merely restating results
expectations.criteria: An honest limitation appears: e.g., benchmark generalizability, single-benchmark scope (HotpotQA), corpus dependence of chunk-size findings, or open questions about real-world vs. benchmark performance.
